video_title: Guy Fieri Devours UNREAL Hot Dogs in Reseda, CA 🌭 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Fab Hot Dogs
restaurants.guy_intro: Time to check out Fab Hot Dogs. I don't eat hot dogs anywhere but Fab's.

dishes.dish_name: The Ripper with Bald Eagle Sauce
dishes.description: Deep-fried hot dog imported from New Jersey, served with a house-made mustard relish sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Mmm. Bald eagle sauce. Money.

dishes.description: All-beef hot dog wrapped in bacon, deep-fried, topped with caramelized onions and peppers, mayo, mustard, ketchup, jalapenos, and tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, man, that's great. Really good crunch. Look at all the juice inside that dog right there. And you can't beat the bacon. The mayo definitely plays in right. It's the right mixture of ingredients. That's a great dog. It's delicious. It's an incredible, like, mix of flavors. How can you possibly avoid a bacon-wrapped hot dog?

restaurants.name: Super Duper Weenie
restaurants.guy_intro: Super Duper's the place to come. Because Gary Zamola is serious about his dogs. A culinary school grad who fell in love with an old hot dog truck.

dishes.description: Hot dog topped with bacon, sauerkraut, spicy brown mustard, sweet cucumber relish, and chopped onions. The relish is house-made with cucumbers, bell peppers, onions, mustard seed, celery seed, sugar, and cider vinegar.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm! Wow, that's good. The hidden surprise in all that is the bacon. Just a little bit of crunch. It just fortifies it. And the sweetness of the relish just takes it all. Where has that been all my life? Never tasted anything like the relish. Best hot dog ever.

restaurants.name: Hillbilly Hot Dogs
restaurants.guy_intro: I've never seen a restaurant like this. When Sonny Knight brought his California-born wife, Sherry, home to West Virginia, they decided to open the ultimate West Virginia hot dog stand. Two school buses stacked up next to a shed.

dishes.description: 15-inch, one-pound all-beef hot dog, deep-fried, topped with jalapenos, habanero sauce, and coleslaw.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, my God, that's hot! It's spicy, but it's good.

dishes.description: Beef hot dog topped with a mixture of ground pork snout and pork belly cooked with bell peppers, jalapenos, and red onions, garlic crema (pureed garlic, garlic powder, salt, sour cream), pickled red onions, sliced red radish, green onions, calamansi, and chicharron.
dishes.guy_response: It eats bigger than pork. Probably any hot dog I've ever had. But that seasick right on top, it's crunchy. It's tender.

dishes.description: Hot dog made from real Florida gator tail meat, ground with pork, fat, vegetables, onion, chili powder, salt, granulated garlic, paprika, cayenne, and white pepper. Served on a bun.
dishes.guy_response: Texturally, it's great. People would probably come in and try it as a novelty. I ate gator. But the level of seasoning and the level of flavor that you have in this, doing it with the peppers and onions makes it a win. It's got a great snap to it.
